mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 4.0%, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in residential housing starts and consumer credit outstanding signal synchronized household balance sheet expansion, which amplifies aggregate demand and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample testing, or SPX 20-day returns show no significant difference when HOUST and RSAFS move in opposite directions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates during an active tightening cycle increase the cost of capital and compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as discount rate effects dominate earnings growth.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day forward returns in over 60% of tightening episodes, or the negative correlation between FEDFUNDS changes and SPX returns breaks down during periods of strong GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production capacity signals robust corporate output and operational leverage, which directly translates into higher earnings per share and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain flat or negative when INDPRO rises for three consecutive months, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after adjusting for CPIAUCSL inflation trends.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth exceeds 2.5 percent, strong aggregate demand validates corporate profit margins and triggers positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are not statistically higher than the median return during sub-2.5% GDP growth periods, or the signal produces false breakouts in more than 40% of backtested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth acceleration and unemployment rate contraction indicate a self-reinforcing labor market boom that fuels wage-push inflation, prompting aggressive Federal Reserve policy responses and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in over 50% of instances where PAYEMS and UNRATE move in the specified direction, or the interaction term shows no incremental explanatory power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained housing starts above 1.2 million units during a late-cycle expansion phase reflect resilient consumer confidence and wealth effects, which support equity valuations and produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns decline when HOUST remains above 1.2 million for two consecutive months, or the hypothesis fails to reject the null of zero predictive ability in a 3-year rolling walk-forward test.
